items.title: Conversation about a research paper
items.ttsText: Narrator. Listen to a conversation between a student and a professor. Student. Professor Adams, do you have a few minutes? I wanted to ask about the research assignment. Professor. Sure, come in. Are you having trouble finding sources? Student. Not exactly. I found several articles, but my topic still feels too broad. Professor. What topic did you choose? Student. I was going to write about the effects of technology on education. Professor. That is a good starting point, but it covers too many areas. You could narrow it down by focusing on one type of technology or one group of students. Student. So, maybe something like how online discussion boards affect participation in college classes? Professor. Yes, that would be much more manageable. You could compare students who participate in online discussions with those who only speak during regular class meetings. Student. That sounds better. Would it be okay if I changed my thesis today? Professor. Absolutely. Revising your thesis at this stage is a normal part of the research process. Narrator. Now answer the questions.

questions.question: Why does the student visit the professor?
choices: To request extra time for an assignment
To ask about narrowing a research topic
To complain about an online class
questions.explanation: The student says the topic feels too broad, so the professor helps narrow it.

questions.question: What is the student's original topic?
choices: The effects of technology on education
The difference between online and offline libraries
questions.explanation: The student says the original topic is the effects of technology on education.

questions.question: What does the professor suggest?
questions.explanation: The professor says the topic covers too many areas and should be narrowed down.

questions.question: What specific topic does the student mention later?
choices: How online discussion boards affect participation
Why college classes should be shorter
questions.explanation: The student suggests focusing on how online discussion boards affect participation in college classes.

items.title: Conversation about a library book
items.ttsText: Narrator. Listen to a conversation between a student and a librarian. Student. Excuse me, I am looking for a book called Introduction to Environmental Policy, but I cannot find it on the shelf. Librarian. Did you check the online catalog first? Student. Yes. It says the book is available, and the call number led me to this section. Librarian. Sometimes books are placed on the reshelving cart before they return to the shelf. Let me check. Student. Thank you. I need it for a paper that is due next week. Librarian. Here it is. Someone returned it this morning. You can borrow it for three weeks. Student. Great. Is it possible to renew it if I need more time? Librarian. Yes, as long as no one else requests it. You can renew it online through your library account. Narrator. Now answer the questions.

questions.question: What problem does the student have?
choices: He cannot access his online account
He cannot find a book on the shelf
He forgot the title of his paper
questions.explanation: The student says he is looking for a book but cannot find it on the shelf.

questions.question: Where was the book found?
questions.explanation: The librarian explains that books are sometimes on the reshelving cart, and then finds the book there.

questions.question: How long can the student borrow the book?
questions.explanation: The librarian says the student can borrow it for three weeks.

items.title: Short talk about campus transportation
items.ttsText: Narrator. Listen to part of an announcement about campus transportation. Speaker. Starting next Monday, the university will introduce a new shuttle schedule. The goal is to reduce waiting times during the busiest hours of the day. Buses will now arrive every ten minutes between seven thirty and nine thirty in the morning, and again between four and six in the afternoon. During the middle of the day, buses will continue to run every twenty minutes. Students are encouraged to check the transportation website because some bus stops will also be moved temporarily due to construction near the science building. The university expects the construction to last about six weeks. Narrator. Now answer the questions.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the announcement?
choices: To explain a new shuttle schedule
To announce a new science course
To ask students to drive to campus
questions.explanation: The speaker announces a new shuttle schedule that starts next Monday.

questions.question: How often will buses arrive during the busiest hours?
questions.explanation: The announcement says buses will arrive every ten minutes during peak hours.

questions.question: Why will some bus stops be moved temporarily?
choices: Because of construction near the science building
Because the buses are being replaced
questions.explanation: The speaker says some bus stops will be moved due to construction near the science building.
